test_spec.name: Calendar Ageing — Storage at 40°C, 50% SOC with Monthly Check-ups
test_spec.description: Calendar ageing study to characterise capacity fade and impedance growth during storage. Cells are fully characterised (C/10 capacity, HPPC, EIS), set to 50% SOC, and stored at 40°C in a climate chamber. Every 30 days the cells are removed, allowed to equilibrate at 25°C for 4 hours, and subjected to a check-up: C/10 capacity measurement and EIS at 50% SOC. After the check-up the cells are returned to 50% SOC and replaced in storage. The protocol runs for up to 12 months or until 80% capacity retention is reached.

notes: Initial characterisation before storage: 3x C/10 cycles for capacity stabilisation, 1x HPPC, 1x EIS at 50% SOC.
Self-discharge is tracked by measuring OCV before and after each storage period.
SOC is set by CC-CV charge to upper voltage limit (C/20 cut-off) then CC discharge at C/5 to the target Ah from nominal capacity.
All EIS check-ups follow protocol test-protocol:2k9p-7t4r-3m6x-5n1v (EIS at 50% SOC, 25°C).
